Client 2: Yeah, same. I’m about five years out, and I don’t want this to get overly complicated.
Advisor: Totally fair. Why don’t we start with the big picture cash flow—income coming in, spending going out—and then we’ll map the accounts and any insurance. Jennifer, what’s your household gross income per year right now, roughly?
Client 1: Household gross is 177100 annually.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. And that’s across both of your jobs plus any other income streams, like rental income?
Client 2: Mostly jobs, and then the rentals, yeah.

Advisor: While I’ve got you—Jennifer, earlier you confirmed the disability policy: monthly premium 600, coverage amount 1.37M, covered until 2037-12-09. Still current, nothing changed recently?

Advisor: Hi Jennifer, hi Daniel—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Thanks. We’re getting close enough to retirement that I want a real plan, not just “we think we’re fine.”
Client 2: Yeah, same. I’m about five years out, and I don’t want this to get overly complicated.

Advisor: Hi Jennifer, hi Daniel—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Thanks. We’re getting close enough to retirement that I want a real plan, not just “we think we’re fine.”

Advisor: Next one is more about how you want to work together. Are you looking for an ongoing relationship where I’m acting as your registered investment advisor—an RIA—or are you just looking for a one-time plan?
Client 1: Ongoing. The advisory channel we want is RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Perfect—thank you. And just so I’m matching your comfort level: when markets get choppy, do you see yourselves as conservative, moderate, or aggressive?
Client 2: I’d say moderate—like, we don’t want big swings.
Advisor: Okay. Jennifer, does “moderate” feel right to you too, or would you put it differently?

Client 1: Yes, those are the main buckets.
Advisor: Okay—thanks. Now I want to zoom out from the accounts for a second and make sure the household basics are right for planning assumptions.

Advisor: Perfect. And in the household, would you say you’re the primary person driving the finances—like the primary role for planning purposes?
Client 1: Yes, my household role is primary.
Advisor: Okay. And what’s your occupation group—broadly speaking? Like finance, tech, operations, that kind of bucket.
Client 1: Operations.
Advisor: Great, thank you. Daniel, I’m not going to drill into your details right now since we’re focused on Jennifer’s fields, but we’ll keep circling back as needed.

Client 1: Yes—monthly premium 600, coverage amount 1.37M, covered until 2037-12-09.
